title: Latent class analyses on attendance of toddler safety promotion intervention
author: George Washington University
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bmsek6be

steps.title: Inclusion criteria A subsample of those...
steps.instruction: Inclusion criteria A subsample of those who were assigned to safety promotion interventiong group (n=91) out of the sample of all the mother-toddler dyads who participated in the a randomized toddler safety promotion intervention trial (n=277). For the mother-toddler dyads who particiated in the intervention trial, the inclusion cirteria are listed below: Inclusion criteria for mothers: age >18 years and not pregnant; for children: age 12–32 months, term birth, birth weight >2500 g, independent ambulation and no congenital or disabling conditions.

steps.title: To assess the patterns of attendance...
steps.instruction: To assess the patterns of attendance among the mother-toddler dyads who participated in a randomized toddler safety promotion intervention trial and were assigned to the safety promotion group based on latent class analyses.

steps.title: To assess the latent class membership...
steps.instruction: To assess the latent class membership of attendance in relation to baseline depressive symptoms.

steps.title: To assess the latent class membership...
steps.instruction: To assess the latent class membership in relation to reduction of home safety problems over time Target variables: Attendence for each of the 8 safety promotion intervention sessions (yes vs. no) Maternal depressive symptoms at baseline Home safety problems at baseline, 6-month and 12-month follow ups.

steps.title: Exclusion criteria
steps.instruction: Exclusion criteria: The mother-toddler dyads who were assigned to the attention control group.

steps.title: Study design Longitudinal with three assessments
steps.instruction: Study design Longitudinal with three assessments: baseline, 6-month follow up and 12-month follow up.

steps.title: The safety promotion prevention intervention trial...
steps.instruction: The safety promotion prevention intervention trial (and also called the Toddler Overweight Prevention STudy among low-Income Families since the attention control group received the obesity prevention) has been registered with the Clinical Trials Registry (NCT02615158). (

steps.title: The University of Maryland Baltimore Institutional...
steps.instruction: The University of Maryland Baltimore Institutional Review Board approved this research; (

steps.title: All mothers provided written consent
steps.instruction: All mothers provided written consent.

steps.title: Surveys / Questionnaires Data on socio-demographic...
steps.instruction: Surveys / Questionnaires Data on socio-demographic characteristics, depressive symptoms were reported by the mothers and the home safety problems were observed by the research assistants.

steps.title: Attendance
steps.instruction: Attendance: Health educators recorded the participants’ attendance at each of the 5 in-person group sessions and in each of the 3 phone calls (1=yes or 0=no).

steps.title: Maternal depressive symptoms
steps.instruction: Maternal depressive symptoms: Maternal depressive symptoms were measured at baseline with the Beck Depression Inventory (BDI)[11]. The BDI includes 21 questions relating to depressive symptoms in the past week, e.g., sadness and feeling guilty, using a 4- point Likert scale ranging from 0 to

steps.title: We calculated a summary score, with...
steps.instruction: We calculated a summary score, with higher scores indicating more symptoms. Cronbach’s alpha was 0.91 in this study, indicating a good internal consistency.

steps.title: Home safety problems:We assessed home safety...
steps.instruction: Home safety problems:We assessed home safety problems at baseline, before the intervention, and at 6- and 12-months after baseline. The summary score was created based on nine items with 1=yes/0=no responses: (

steps.title: child-resistant latches on cleaning supply/medication cabinets [8]
steps.instruction: child-resistant latches on cleaning supply/medication cabinets [8]. The summary score ranges from 0 to 9, with a high score indicating more safety problems.

steps.title: Latent class analyses to identify the...
steps.instruction: Latent class analyses to identify the different pattern in the attendance of safety promotion intervention.

steps.title: Logistic regression (or multinomial logistic regression)...
steps.instruction: Logistic regression (or multinomial logistic regression) to assess the baseline predictors of latent class membership.

steps.title: Linear mixed model to assess the...
steps.instruction: Linear mixed model to assess the difference in the reductio of home safety problems over time between the two classes.
